title: Apply for citizenship: Calculate your physical presence - Canada.ca
content:
## Apply for citizenship: Calculate your physical presence
Answer the following question to learn how to calculate your physical presence (opens in a new tab) .
The calculator is now available in the online account (opens in a new tab) .
- You can only use the calculator after you create your account.
- To do your calculation, follow the instructions in the “ Physical presence calculator ” section of the online account.
Before you apply online, you can check if you have at least 1,095 days of physical presence with the calculator for paper application (opens in a new tab) .
You must still use the calculator in your account to apply online.

## Track your time with the travel journal (optional)
You may use our travel journal to record your trips outside Canada.
- This helps you calculate how many days you’ve been in Canada.
- You don’t need to include the travel journal in your application.
Get the travel journal (opens in a new tab)
You have 2 options to do your calculation:

### Option 1: Use the online physical presence calculator (recommended)
How to use the online calculator:
- Fill out the calculator.
- Print your completed calculation.
- Include the calculation printout in your application.
Go to the calculator (opens in a new tab)

### Option 2: Complete the CIT 0407 form
If you can’t or don’t want to use the online physical presence calculator
- fill out and print this form: How to Calculate Physical Presence (CIT 0407) (opens in new tab)
- include the completed form in your application

## Track your time with the travel journal (optional)
You may use our travel journal to record your trips outside Canada.
- This helps you calculate how many days you’ve been in Canada.
- You don’t need to include the travel journal in your application.
Get the travel journal (opens in a new tab)
